#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Requires: Brave launched with --remote-debugging-port=9222

PORT=9222

if ! curl -sf "http://localhost:$PORT/json" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    rofi -e "tabs: Brave not running with remote debugging.\nRestart Brave with: --remote-debugging-port=$PORT"
    exit 1
fi

tabs=$(curl -sf "http://localhost:$PORT/json" \
    | jq -r '.[] | select(.type == "page") | "\(.id)\t\(.title)\t\(.url)"')

[ -z "$tabs" ] && rofi -e "tabs: no open tabs found" && exit 0

chosen=$(echo "$tabs" \
    | awk -F'\t' '{printf "%-50s %s\n", $2, $3}' \
    | rofi -dmenu -p "tabs" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi)
[ -z "$chosen" ] && exit 0

title=$(echo "$chosen" | awk '{$NF=""; sub(/ +$/, ""); print}' | xargs)
tab_id=$(echo "$tabs" | awk -F'\t' -v t="$title" 'index($2, t) {print $1; exit}')

[ -z "$tab_id" ] && exit 0

curl -sf -X POST "http://localhost:$PORT/json/activate/$tab_id" > /dev/null
xdotool search --name "Brave" windowactivate --sync 2>/dev/null || true

content.text:
The issue is on line 27. `xdotool windowactivate` activates the window but doesn't switch to the AwesomeWM tag where Brave lives. You need to switch the tag first.

The fix is to use `wmctrl -ia` which sends `_NET_ACTIVE_WINDOW` properly and tells AwesomeWM to switch to the tag containing the window:

input.old_string:
curl -sf -X POST "http://localhost:$PORT/json/activate/$tab_id" > /dev/null
xdotool search --name "Brave" windowactivate --sync 2>/dev/null || true
input.new_string:
curl -sf -X POST "http://localhost:$PORT/json/activate/$tab_id" > /dev/null

win_id=$(xdotool search --class "brave-browser" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
if [ -n "$win_id" ]; then
    wmctrl -ia "$win_id"
else
    wmctrl -a "Brave" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Requires: Brave launched with --remote-debugging-port=9222

PORT=9222

if ! curl -sf "http://localhost:$PORT/json" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    rofi -e "tabs: Brave not running with remote debugging.\nRestart Brave with: --remote-debugging-port=$PORT"
    exit 1
fi

tabs=$(curl -sf "http://localhost:$PORT/json" \
    | jq -r '.[] | select(.type == "page") | "\(.id)\t\(.title)\t\(.url)"')

[ -z "$tabs" ] && rofi -e "tabs: no open tabs found" && exit 0

chosen=$(echo "$tabs" \
    | awk -F'\t' '{printf "%-50s %s\n", $2, $3}' \
    | rofi -dmenu -p "tabs" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi)
[ -z "$chosen" ] && exit 0

title=$(echo "$chosen" | awk '{$NF=""; sub(/ +$/, ""); print}' | xargs)
tab_id=$(echo "$tabs" | awk -F'\t' -v t="$title" 'index($2, t) {print $1; exit}')

[ -z "$tab_id" ] && exit 0

curl -sf -X POST "http://localhost:$PORT/json/activate/$tab_id" > /dev/null
xdotool search --name "Brave" windowactivate --sync 2>/dev/null || true

content.text: `wmctrl -ia <window_id>` sends the EWMH `_NET_ACTIVE_WINDOW` message with the proper source flag, which AwesomeWM respects to switch tags. Make sure `wmctrl` is installed (`pacman -S wmctrl` if not).

content.text:
You've got solid coverage already. Here's what could complement your setup well, based on what you're missing:

**Productivity**
- **`pass`** — password manager picker using `pass` or `rbw` (bitwarden CLI), type/copy credentials
- **`notes`** — quick-capture a note to a file, or browse/search existing notes
- **`kill`** — fuzzy pick a running process to kill (like a GUI task manager)
- **`todo`** — add/check off items from a plain text todo file

**Dev workflow (fits your existing style)**
- **`git-branch`** — list branches across your projects, checkout one in a new terminal
- **`man`** — fuzzy search man pages, open selected in terminal
- **`env`** — switch between `.env` files or profiles for a project (e.g. dev/staging/prod)

**System**
- **`mount`** — list unmounted drives/USB devices, mount selected one
- **`screenshot`** — pick mode (full, window, region) then trigger `maim`/`scrot`
- **`notify`** — set a one-off timer reminder ("in 10 minutes: call back X")
- **`brightness`** / **`volume`** — quick slider-style picker if you don't have hardware keys

**AwesomeWM-specific**
- **`window`** — list all open windows across all tags, jump to one (like `tabs` but for X windows) — useful complement to `tabs` since it fixes the same tag-switching gap for other apps

The most useful ones given your setup are probably **`kill`**, **`window`** (tag-aware window switcher), and **`pass`**. Want me to write any of these?

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Requires: nmcli (networkmanager)

connected=$(nmcli -t -f active,ssid dev wifi | grep "^yes" | cut -d: -f2)

options=$(nmcli -f SSID,SIGNAL,SECURITY dev wifi list 2>/dev/null \
    | tail -n +2 \
    | awk '{printf "%-35s %s%% %s\n", $1, $2, $3}' \
    | sort -t'%' -k1 -rn)

chosen=$(printf "disconnect\n%s" "$options" \
    | rofi -dmenu -p "wifi [${connected:-none}]" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi \
    | awk '{print $1}')

[ -z "$chosen" ] && exit 0

content.text: `speedtest-cli` is broken — it's a known issue with their servers. The simplest fix that needs no extra install is to use `curl` against a public test server:

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Uses curl against public test servers — no extra deps

action=$(printf "run speedtest\ncancel" \
    | rofi -dmenu -p "speedtest" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi)

[ -z "$action" ] || [ "$action" = "cancel" ] && exit 0

notify-send "Speedtest" "Running... (~15s)" 2>/dev/null || true

# Download: fetch 100MB file, measure speed
down_bps=$(curl -o /dev/null -s -w "%{speed_download}" \
    --max-time 10 "https://speed.cloudflare.com/__down?bytes=20000000")
down=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f Mbit/s\", $down_bps * 8 / 1000000}")

# Upload: send 10MB of data
up_bps=$(curl -s -w "%{speed_upload}" -o /dev/null \
    --max-time 10 -X POST \
    -H "Content-Type: application/octet-stream" \
    --data-binary "$(dd if=/dev/urandom bs=1M count=5 2>/dev/null)" \
    "https://speed.cloudflare.com/__up")
up=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f Mbit/s\", $up_bps * 8 / 1000000}")

# Ping
ping_ms=$(ping -c 4 1.1.1.1 2>/dev/null \
    | awk -F'/' '/rtt/ {printf "%.2f ms", $5}')
[ -z "$ping_ms" ] && ping_ms="n/a"

rofi -e "$(printf 'Download:  %s\nUpload:    %s\nPing:      %s' "$down" "$up" "$ping_ms")" \
    -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Opens a git project in kitty + nvim
# Edit PROJECT_DIRS to your paths

PROJECT_DIRS=(
    "$HOME"
    "$HOME/projects"
    "$HOME/dev"
    "$HOME/work"
)

projects=$(find "${PROJECT_DIRS[@]}" -maxdepth 3 -name ".git" -type d 2>/dev/null \
    | sed 's|/.git||' \
    | sort)

[ -z "$projects" ] && rofi -e "No git projects found" && exit 1

chosen=$(echo "$projects" | rofi -dmenu -p "project" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi)
[ -z "$chosen" ] && exit 0

kitty --directory "$chosen" nvim &

    | rofi -dmenu -p "speedtest" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi)

    server_json=$(curl -s -A "Mozilla/5.0" \
        "https://www.speedtest.net/api/js/servers?engine=js&https_functional=true&limit=1")
    server_url=$(echo "$server_json" | jq -r ".[0].url")
    server_name=$(echo "$server_json" | jq -r ".[0].name + \" (\" + .[0].sponsor + \")\"")

    echo ""

    echo -n "Ping... "
    ping_ms=$(ping -c 4 "$server_host" 2>/dev/null | awk -F"/" "/rtt/ {printf \"%.2f ms\", \$5}")

    echo -n "Download... "
    down_bps=$(curl -o /dev/null -s -w "%{speed_download}" \
        --max-time 20 "$base_url/download?size=25000000")

    printf "%.2f Mbit/s\n" "$(echo "$up_bps" | awk "{print \$1 * 8 / 1000000}")"

    echo "Press any key to close..."
    read -n1
' &

content.text: fast.com has a simple API we can hit with curl. The trick is extracting their token first:

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Uses fast.com API via curl — requires jq

action=$(printf "run speedtest\ncancel" \
    | rofi -dmenu -p "speedtest" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi)

[ -z "$action" ] || [ "$action" = "cancel" ] && exit 0

kitty --title "speedtest" bash -c '
    echo "=== fast.com speedtest ==="
    echo ""

    echo -n "Getting token... "
    js_url=$(curl -s "https://fast.com/" | grep -oP "src=\"/app-[^\"]+\.js\"" | head -1 | sed "s/src=\x22//;s/\x22//")
    token=$(curl -s "https://fast.com${js_url}" | grep -oP "token:\x22[^\x22]+\x22" | head -1 | grep -oP "\x22[^\x22]+\x22" | tr -d "\x22")

    if [ -z "$token" ]; then
        echo "failed"
        echo "Could not retrieve fast.com token"
        read -n1
        exit 1
    fi
    echo "ok"

    echo -n "Getting servers... "
    urls=$(curl -s "https://api.fast.com/netflix/speedtest/v2?https=true&token=${token}&urlCount=3" \
        | jq -r ".targets[].url")

    if [ -z "$urls" ]; then
        echo "failed"
        read -n1
        exit 1
    fi
    echo "ok"
    echo ""

    echo -n "Download... "
    down_bps=$(echo "$urls" | head -1 | xargs -I{} curl -o /dev/null -s -w "%{speed_download}" --max-time 20 "{}")
    printf "%.2f Mbit/s\n" "$(echo "$down_bps" | awk "{print \$1 * 8 / 1000000}")"

    echo -n "Upload... "
    up_bps=$(dd if=/dev/urandom bs=1M count=10 2>/dev/null \
        | curl -s -w "%{speed_upload}" -o /dev/null \
            --max-time 20 -X POST \
            -H "Content-Type: application/octet-stream" \
            --data-binary @- \
            "$(echo "$urls" | head -1)")
    printf "%.2f Mbit/s\n" "$(echo "$up_bps" | awk "{print \$1 * 8 / 1000000}")"

    echo ""
    echo "Press any key to close..."
    read -n1
' &

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Uses fast.com API via curl — requires jq

action=$(printf "run speedtest\ncancel" \
    | rofi -dmenu -p "speedtest" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi)

[ -z "$action" ] || [ "$action" = "cancel" ] && exit 0

js_url=$(curl -s "https://fast.com/" | grep -oP 'src="/app-[^"]+\.js"' | head -1 | sed 's/src="//;s/"//')
token=$(curl -s "https://fast.com${js_url}" | grep -oP 'token:"[^"]+"' | head -1 | tr -d '"' | sed 's/token://')

kitty --title "speedtest" bash -c '
    token="'"$token"'"
    echo "=== fast.com speedtest ==="
    echo ""

    echo -n "Getting servers... "
    urls=$(curl -s "https://api.fast.com/netflix/speedtest/v2?https=true&token=${token}&urlCount=3" \
        | jq -r ".targets[].url")

    if [ -z "$urls" ]; then
        echo "failed"
        read -n1
        exit 1
    fi
    echo "ok"
    echo ""

    echo -n "Download... "
    down_bps=$(echo "$urls" | head -1 | xargs -I{} curl -o /dev/null -s -w "%{speed_download}" --max-time 20 "{}")
    printf "%.2f Mbit/s\n" "$(echo "$down_bps" | awk "{print \$1 * 8 / 1000000}")"

    echo -n "Upload... "
    up_bps=$(dd if=/dev/urandom bs=1M count=10 2>/dev/null \
        | curl -s -w "%{speed_upload}" -o /dev/null \
            --max-time 20 -X POST \
            -H "Content-Type: application/octet-stream" \
            --data-binary @- \
            "$(echo "$urls" | head -1)")
    printf "%.2f Mbit/s\n" "$(echo "$up_bps" | awk "{print \$1 * 8 / 1000000}")"

    echo ""
    echo "Press any key to close..."
    read -n1
' &

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Requires: translate-shell (sudo pacman -S translate-shell)

if ! command -v trans &>/dev/null; then
    rofi -e "translate: install translate-shell (sudo pacman -S translate-shell)"
    exit 1
fi

langs="en\nfr\nes\nde\nit\npt\nzh\nja\nar\nru"

target=$(printf "$langs" \
    | rofi -dmenu -p "translate to" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi)
[ -z "$target" ] && exit 0

text=$(rofi -dmenu -p "text" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi < /dev/null)
[ -z "$text" ] && exit 0

result=$(trans -b ":${target}" "$text" 2>/dev/null)
[ -z "$result" ] && rofi -e "translate: no result" && exit 1

echo "$result" | xclip -selection clipboard 2>/dev/null || true

rofi -e "$(printf '%s\n\n(copied to clipboard)' "$result")" \
    -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi

if ! command -v trans &>/dev/null; then
    rofi -e "translate: install translate-shell (sudo pacman -S translate-shell)"
    exit 1
fi

langs="en\nfr\nes\nde\nit\npt\nzh\nja\nar\nru"

target=$(printf "$langs" \
    | rofi -dmenu -p "translate to" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi)
[ -z "$target" ] && exit 0

text=$(rofi -dmenu -p "text" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi < /dev/null)
[ -z "$text" ] && exit 0

result=$(trans -b ":${target}" "$text" 2>/dev/null)
[ -z "$result" ] && rofi -e "translate: no result" && exit 1

echo "$result" | xclip -selection clipboard 2>/dev/null || true

rofi -e "$(printf '%s\n\n(copied to clipboard)' "$result")" \
    -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Requires: tmux (sudo pacman -S tmux)

if ! command -v tmux &>/dev/null; then
    rofi -e "tmux: install tmux (sudo pacman -S tmux)"
    exit 1
fi

sessions=$(tmux ls 2>/dev/null | awk -F: '{print $1}')
new_option="+ new session"

chosen=$({ [ -n "$sessions" ] && echo "$sessions"; echo "$new_option"; } \
    | rofi -dmenu -p "tmux" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi)
[ -z "$chosen" ] && exit 0

if [ "$chosen" = "$new_option" ]; then
    name=$(rofi -dmenu -p "session name" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi <<< "")
    [ -z "$name" ] && exit 0
    kitty tmux new-session -s "$name" &
else
    kitty tmux attach-session -t "$chosen" &
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Requires: tmux (sudo pacman -S tmux)

if ! command -v tmux &>/dev/null; then
    rofi -e "tmux: install tmux (sudo pacman -S tmux)"
    exit 1
fi

sessions=$(tmux ls 2>/dev/null | awk -F: '{print $1}')

chosen=$({ [ -n "$sessions" ] && echo "$sessions"; echo "+ new session"; } \
    | rofi -dmenu -p "tmux" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi)
[ -z "$chosen" ] && exit 0

if [ "$chosen" = "+ new session" ]; then
    name=$(rofi -dmenu -p "session name" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi <<< "")
    [ -z "$name" ] && exit 0
    kitty tmux new-session -s "$name" &
    exit 0
fi

action=$(printf "attach\nkill\nrename\nnew window\nlist windows" \
    | rofi -dmenu -p "$chosen" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi)
[ -z "$action" ] && exit 0

case "$action" in
    attach)
        kitty tmux attach-session -t "$chosen" &
        ;;
    kill)
        confirm=$(printf "yes\nno" \
            | rofi -dmenu -p "kill $chosen?" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi)
        [ "$confirm" = "yes" ] && tmux kill-session -t "$chosen" \
            && notify-send "tmux" "Session '$chosen' killed"
        ;;
    rename)
        newname=$(rofi -dmenu -p "rename to" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi <<< "")
        [ -z "$newname" ] && exit 0
        tmux rename-session -t "$chosen" "$newname" \
            && notify-send "tmux" "Renamed '$chosen' to '$newname'"
        ;;
    "new window")
        winname=$(rofi -dmenu -p "window name" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi <<< "")
        [ -z "$winname" ] && exit 0
        tmux new-window -t "$chosen" -n "$winname"
        kitty tmux attach-session -t "$chosen" &
        ;;
    "list windows")
        windows=$(tmux list-windows -t "$chosen" \
            | awk -F: '{print $1 ":" $2}' | sed 's/^ //')
        [ -z "$windows" ] && rofi -e "tmux: no windows in $chosen" && exit 1
        win=$(echo "$windows" \
            | rofi -dmenu -p "$chosen windows" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi)
        [ -z "$win" ] && exit 0
        win_idx=$(echo "$win" | awk -F: '{print $1}')
        kitty tmux attach-session -t "$chosen" \; select-window -t "$win_idx" &
        ;;
esac

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Requires: tmux (sudo pacman -S tmux)

if ! command -v tmux &>/dev/null; then
    rofi -e "tmux: install tmux (sudo pacman -S tmux)"
    exit 1
fi

sessions=$(tmux ls 2>/dev/null | awk -F: '{print $1}')

chosen=$({ [ -n "$sessions" ] && echo "$sessions"; echo "+ new session"; } \
    | rofi -dmenu -p "tmux" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi)
[ -z "$chosen" ] && exit 0

if [ "$chosen" = "+ new session" ]; then
    name=$(rofi -dmenu -p "session name" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi <<< "")
    [ -z "$name" ] && exit 0
    kitty tmux new-session -s "$name" &
    exit 0
fi

action=$(printf "+ attach\n+ new window\n+ list windows\n+ rename\n+ kill" \
    | rofi -dmenu -p "$chosen" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi)
[ -z "$action" ] && exit 0

case "$action" in
    "+ attach")
        kitty tmux attach-session -t "$chosen" &
        ;;
    "+ kill")
        confirm=$(printf "yes\nno" \
            | rofi -dmenu -p "kill $chosen?" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi)
        [ "$confirm" = "yes" ] && tmux kill-session -t "$chosen" \
            && notify-send "tmux" "Session '$chosen' killed"
        ;;
    "+ rename")
        newname=$(rofi -dmenu -p "rename to" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi <<< "")
        [ -z "$newname" ] && exit 0
        tmux rename-session -t "$chosen" "$newname" \
            && notify-send "tmux" "Renamed '$chosen' to '$newname'"
        ;;
    "+ new window")
        winname=$(rofi -dmenu -p "window name" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi <<< "")
        [ -z "$winname" ] && exit 0
        tmux new-window -t "$chosen" -n "$winname"
        kitty tmux attach-session -t "$chosen" &
        ;;
    "+ list windows")
        windows=$(tmux list-windows -t "$chosen" \
            | awk -F: '{print $1 ":" $2}' | sed 's/^ //')
        [ -z "$windows" ] && rofi -e "tmux: no windows in $chosen" && exit 1
        win=$(echo "$windows" \
            | rofi -dmenu -p "$chosen windows" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi)
        [ -z "$win" ] && exit 0
        win_idx=$(echo "$win" | awk -F: '{print $1}')
        kitty tmux attach-session -t "$chosen" \; select-window -t "$win_idx" &
        ;;
esac

content.text: The timing issue persists. Let me avoid it entirely by combining sessions and actions into a single rofi call:

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Requires: tmux (sudo pacman -S tmux)

if ! command -v tmux &>/dev/null; then
    rofi -e "tmux: install tmux (sudo pacman -S tmux)"
    exit 1
fi

sessions=$(tmux ls 2>/dev/null | awk -F: '{print $1}')

menu="+ new session"
for s in $sessions; do
    menu="$menu\n$s\n+ kill: $s\n+ rename: $s\n+ new window in: $s"
done

chosen=$(printf "$menu" \
    | rofi -dmenu -p "tmux" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi)
[ -z "$chosen" ] && exit 0

case "$chosen" in
    "+ new session")
        name=$(rofi -dmenu -p "session name" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi <<< "")
        [ -z "$name" ] && exit 0
        kitty tmux new-session -s "$name" &
        ;;
    "+ kill: "*)
        s="${chosen#+ kill: }"
        confirm=$(printf "yes\nno" \
            | rofi -dmenu -p "kill $s?" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi)
        [ "$confirm" = "yes" ] && tmux kill-session -t "$s" \
            && notify-send "tmux" "Session '$s' killed"
        ;;
    "+ rename: "*)
        s="${chosen#+ rename: }"
        newname=$(rofi -dmenu -p "rename $s to" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi <<< "")
        [ -z "$newname" ] && exit 0
        tmux rename-session -t "$s" "$newname" \
            && notify-send "tmux" "Renamed '$s' to '$newname'"
        ;;
    "+ new window in: "*)
        s="${chosen#+ new window in: }"
        winname=$(rofi -dmenu -p "window name" -config ~/.config/rofi/config.rasi <<< "")
        [ -z "$winname" ] && exit 0
        tmux new-window -t "$s" -n "$winname"
        kitty tmux attach-session -t "$s" &
        ;;
    *)
        kitty tmux attach-session -t "$chosen" &
        ;;
esac

# BEGIN_KITTY_THEME
# Monokai Classic
include current-theme.conf

font_size 10.0

shell /usr/bin/fish --login

# Cursor customization
cursor_text_color #111111
cursor_shape underline

# Scrollback
scrollback_lines -1

# Mouse
open_url_with default
copy_on_select yes
url_prefixes file ftp ftps gemini git gopher http https irc ircs kitty mailto news sftp ssh
detect_urls yes
strip_trailing_spaces smart
focus_follows_mouse no

# Performance tuning
sync_to_monitor yes

# Terminal Bell
bell_on_tab "🔔 "

# Color scheme
# background_opacity 0.9

# Advanced
allow_remote_control yes
allow_hyperlinks yes
#macos_hide_titlebar yes
hide_window_decorations yes

#kitten
map cmd+z launch --allow-remote-control kitty +kitten kitty_search/search.py @active-kitty-window-id

map cmd+g kitten kitty_grab/grab.py
# END_KITTY_THEME

# BEGIN_KITTY_FONTS
font_family      family="DejaVu Sans Mono"
bold_font        auto
italic_font      auto
bold_italic_font auto
# END_KITTY_FONTS

content.text:
Config is clean and minimal. A few observations:

**Good choices**
- `scrollback_lines -1` — unlimited scrollback, great for dev work
- `copy_on_select yes` — efficient
- `allow_remote_control yes` — needed for the rofi scripts that use kitty
- `hide_window_decorations yes` — clean look
- Everforest Dark theme — solid, easy on the eyes (matches your rofi `#A7C080` accent too)

**Things worth considering**

1. **Font** — you're using `DejaVu Sans Mono` but you have `JetBrainsMono NF` in your rofi config. You could use JetBrainsMono in kitty too for consistency, and get Nerd Font icons in the terminal
2. **`cmd+z` / `cmd+g` keybinds** — you're on Linux/AwesomeWM, `cmd` is the Super key. These may not fire as expected — `ctrl+z` or `ctrl+g` would be more standard
3. **`scrollback_lines -1`** — unlimited is fine but can eat memory on very verbose processes. `100000` is a practical cap most people never hit
4. **`sync_to_monitor yes`** — good for tearing prevention, but if you ever feel input lag, try disabling it

**Missing things you might want**
- `cursor_blink_interval` — underline cursor without blink can be hard to spot
- `tab_bar_style` — if you ever use kitty tabs alongside tmux
- `background_opacity` — you have it commented out, might be worth enabling with AwesomeWM

Want me to apply any of these?

message.content: ok do all and explain me this is for what cmd+z / cmd+g keybinds

content.text:
**`cmd+z` / `cmd+g` explained:**
- `cmd+z` — opens `kitty_search`, a kitten that lets you search text in your scrollback buffer (like Ctrl+F in a browser)
- `cmd+g` — opens `kitty_grab`, a kitten for keyboard-driven text selection (vim-like motions to select and copy text without the mouse)

Both are useful but `cmd` on Linux = Super key, which AwesomeWM already uses as its main modifier — so they likely never fire. I'll remap them to `ctrl+shift`.
